_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an average price index of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice benchmarked to 2015 (=100), currently stands at 141, indicating a 41% rise since 2015. This increase is uneven across commodities and has occurred relatively recently rather than steadily over the years. The basket provides a composite view of staple soft commodities relevant to global consumers. It reflects aggregations of raw commodity costs rather than retail prices, focusing on market-level data.
notes.movers: Coffee prices have nearly doubled since 2015, up 99%, making it the largest price mover in the basket. Orange juice has also increased notably by 34%, while wheat and sugar have risen more modestly by 19% and 13%, respectively. These changes are largely attributable to weather conditions and crop diseases affecting soft commodity markets, such as frost or drought in arabica coffee growing regions and citrus greening impacting orange juice production. Soft commodity markets tend to be relatively thin, meaning smaller volumes and more price volatility due to supply shocks.
notes.why: The headline rise in the breakfast basket is driven by a handful of weather-sensitive and disease-impacted crops rather than broad-based inflationary pressures. Because supply risks and production challenges persist in these key soft commodities, elevated input prices have the potential to remain for several years. This underscores the importance of monitoring climatic and biological factors in forecasting commodity trends.
